skill.name: emailautomationskill
skill.description: Helps AI agents plan, audit, and improve email automation workflows with trigger checks, lifecycle governance, QA steps, and approval gates.
skill.summary: Plan and review email automation workflows with clear safeguards before live changes.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed all four static weak-cryptography alerts as false positives. The matches occur in ordinary documentation text, not cryptographic code, executable logic, network access, or secret handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The alert on the OpenAI agent metadata points to normal descriptive text. No cryptographic function, key handling, or insecure algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 2 is a short description of the skill. It contains no code, algorithm selection, cryptographic material, or execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The alert on the operating checklist points to risk-gate prose about high-risk email operations. No cryptographic operation or weak algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 27 describes live sends, imports, suppression edits, DNS changes, and destructive cleanup. It is governance text, not crypto usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The alert on the skill description points to plain-language activation guidance. No cryptographic function, hash, cipher, or insecure algorithm appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML front matter describing when to use the skill. It contains no executable logic or cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The alert on the platform boundary points to documentation guidance for provider-specific steps. No weak cryptographic algorithm is used or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 22 instructs agents to describe platform steps in operational language. It contains documentation guidance only.

content.value_statement: Email automations can create duplicate sends, stale journeys, or risky production edits. This skill guides agents through trigger review, QA planning, governance checks, and approval gates.
actual_capabilities: Drafts journey contracts with triggers, eligibility, waits, branches, exits, suppression, and success events.
Builds trigger inventories for behavioral, lifecycle, transactional, reactivation, operational, and sales-assisted automations.
Creates QA plans that cover test contacts, branch checks, stale contacts, suppression rules, event delays, and rollback steps.
Audits overlapping automations for duplicate sends, contradictory offers, and frequency-cap pressure.
Recommends governance rules for naming, ownership, change logs, review cadence, and approval gates.
Separates recommendations from live actions and requires explicit approval for high-risk production changes.
limitations: Does not connect to email platforms or make live production changes by itself.
Requires source material, exports, screenshots, or metrics to support specific recommendations.
Does not assume consent, suppression state, or missing customer data fields.
Provider-specific steps remain operational guidance unless platform documentation is supplied.

use_cases.title: Review a Lifecycle Journey
use_cases.description: Map triggers, waits, branches, exits, suppression rules, and success events before changing an active lifecycle flow.

use_cases.title: Prepare Automation QA
use_cases.description: Create a QA checklist with test contacts, event-delay checks, stale-contact controls, and rollback notes.

use_cases.title: Govern Agent Handoffs
use_cases.description: Produce clear ownership, approval gates, and change-log expectations before another agent edits an email workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Journey Contract
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a structured automation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a journey contract for a welcome automation. Include trigger, eligibility, waits, exits, suppression, success event, and approval gates.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Trigger Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of existing automation triggers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review these automation notes and identify missing triggers, stale-flow risks, overlap issues, and required evidence for each recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Production QA Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced preparation before a live journey change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a QA plan for this lifecycle journey. Include test contacts, branch checks, event-delay risks, rollback steps, and approval owners.

prompt_templates.title: Design Governance for Multiple Automations
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced governance across several active workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit these active automations for duplicate sends, contradictory offers, frequency pressure, missing owners, and governance gaps.

output_examples.input: Review this abandoned-cart automation before activation.
output: Journey contract with trigger, eligibility, wait steps, exit rules, suppression checks, and success event.
QA checklist covering branch tests, delayed events, stale contacts, and rollback readiness.
Approval notes that separate recommendations from activation.

output_examples.input: Find risks in our lifecycle email flows.
output: Trigger inventory grouped by lifecycle stage and owner.
Overlap audit for duplicate sends, conflicting offers, and frequency-cap pressure.
Governance recommendations for naming, change logs, scheduled review, and human approval.

best_practices: Provide exports, screenshots, metrics, and platform constraints before asking for recommendations.
Name the owner and approval path for every production automation change.
Validate consent, suppression, frequency caps, and rollback steps before activation.
anti_patterns: Asking the agent to activate, import, suppress, or send without explicit human approval.
Relying on vague intent instead of concrete behavioral triggers and success events.
Changing live journeys without test contacts, rollback notes, and change logs.

faq.question: Can this skill send emails?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning, review, QA, and governance guidance. Live sends require explicit human approval and platform access.

faq.question: Which email platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It gives platform-neutral operational guidance. Provider-specific steps should be supported by platform documentation or user-provided context.

faq.question: Can it audit existing automations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review triggers, branches, waits, exits, suppression rules, metrics, overlap risks, and governance gaps.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Share goals, owner, trigger source, workflow screenshots, branch exports, suppression rules, frequency caps, metrics, and approval process.

faq.question: Does it handle consent and suppression rules?
faq.answer: It checks that consent and suppression rules are addressed, but it does not assume missing customer data or legal status.

faq.question: Is it suitable for AI agent handoffs?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes clear evidence, ownership, next steps, approval gates, and separation between recommendations and live actions.
